summary: Coder addressed all 5 v1 findings correctly, but the MemoryStack.Clone() fix introduces a type-slicing regression for PathData and IdentityData. These Data subclasses don't override Clone(), so they get cloned into plain Data objects — losing filesystem references, cached path properties, and identity fields. Fix: either override Clone() in PathData/IdentityData, or share non-overriding subclasses by reference.

previous_reviews_assessed.codeanalyzer: n/a — no new review since v1
previous_reviews_assessed.tester: n/a — no new review since v1
previous_reviews_assessed.security: n/a — no new review since v1

findings.issue: MemoryStack.Clone() changed from `GetType() != typeof(Data)` (share all subclasses by ref) to `is SettingsData` (share only SettingsData by ref). This means PathData and IdentityData now go through Clone(). Neither overrides Clone(), so Data.Clone() creates `new Data(Name, clonedValue, _type)` — a plain Data. PathData loses _fs, _absolutePath, Relative, Extension, etc. IdentityData loses PublicKey, PrivateKey, IsDefault, IsArchived.
findings.impact: After MemoryStack.Clone(), variables that were PathData become plain Data — all PathData-specific property navigation (%file.Extension%, %file.Relative%) stops working. Same for IdentityData. This affects any code path where a handler stores PathData/IdentityData in a return variable and then a sub-goal is called (which clones context).
findings.suggestion: Two options: (a) Override Clone() in PathData and IdentityData to preserve their type. (b) Change MemoryStack.Clone() to check if the subclass overrides Clone() — if not, share by reference. Option (a) is cleanest and aligns with the virtual Clone() pattern already established by DataList<T>.
